title: GDPR minimization vs SOC 2 audit retention
arguments: Global SaaS with EU users needs to reconcile GDPR data minimization and storage limitation with SOC 2 evidence retention and audit logging requirements. --as-of 2026-02-15 --jurisdictions EU,US --industry saas --strict-facts

title: HIPAA record retention vs GDPR erasure rights
arguments: US healthcare SaaS serving EU users must reconcile HIPAA documentation retention expectations with GDPR data subject deletion rights. --as-of 2026-02-15 --jurisdictions US,EU --industry healthcare-saas --strict-facts

title: PCI DSS evidentiary logging vs GDPR minimization
arguments: Payments SaaS for EU customers must preserve PCI DSS evidentiary logging while minimizing personal data retention under GDPR and maintaining SOC 2 control consistency. --as-of 2026-02-15 --jurisdictions EU,US --industry ecommerce-payments --strict-facts

title: CCPA and GDPR rights with SOC 2 evidence needs
arguments: Global SaaS handling EU and California user data needs to satisfy deletion and correction rights while preserving compliance evidence for audits. --as-of 2026-02-15 --jurisdictions EU,US_CA --industry saas --strict-facts

title: DORA and GDPR incident timeline harmonization
arguments: EU fintech platform must align DORA operational incident reporting with GDPR personal data breach timelines without duplicate response workflows. --as-of 2026-02-15 --jurisdictions EU --industry fintech --strict-facts
